title: Leuzea froedinii
intro: Leuzea froedinii, also known as African Rhaponticum or Snow Lotus, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Eastern Africa. It has been traditionally used for its purported health benefits, including improved physical performance, reduced fatigue, and enhanced immune function. The bioactive constituents of Leuzea froedinii include a group of sesquiterpene lactones, specifically leuzein, leucodin, and leucoselin, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. Leuzea froedinii is typically used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are often marketed as adaptogenic supplements to promote overall well-being and resilience.
